title: CJNF
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:> The Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun}} __NOTOC__ {{User:The Bishop in the Blue Dimension/Template:New Fun Theme}}{{User:The Bishop in the Blue Dimension/Template:Wikipedia Assets}}{{CSS|content= .rail-module { background:transparent; } }} [[File:New Fun Flag.png|link=|center|450px]]
''EX CREPUSCULO AURORA.''
"From dusk cometh dawn."
== Description == The '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' ('''CJNF'''), also alluded to as the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' or '''New Fun''' (not to be confused with [[New Fun|the sub-level]]), is a [[Groups|group]] in [[the Backrooms]] which proclaims itself as an independent sovereign state. It is located in [[New Fun]], with an estimated population of 37,200 non-human citizens known as [[New Fun|celebrants]]. The nation was founded by [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]], otherwise known as the Party Creator, and establishes itself as "the truer, purer form of the [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoer Jamboree]]", promoting freedom of speech and action amongst its denizens, whilst simultaneously maintaining a simple governmental system and legislative and medical services to prevent the descent of the state into complete anarchy. Its government is purported as a parliamentary ''laissez-faire'' jamboree.A jamboree is a system of government declaring to prioritise hedonism, joy and personal freedom in their people. '''New Fun''' settlements have been established all across [[New Fun]], with its capital and largest colony, '''New Fun City''', boasting a population of approximately 5,420 celebrants. At present, there are around 150 known cities in [[New Fun]] under the jurisdiction of New Fun, as well as many smaller towns and municipalities; however, outside their native sub-level, New Fun bases are far and few between, and only four are known to exist, two of which are stated to be located somewhere in [[Level Fun]]. Despite the insistence of the '''New Fun''' people that they should be markedly distinguished from the [[partygoers]], most polities within [[the Backrooms]] still treat the Celebrants' Jamboree and the Partygoer Jamboree in a similar political light, fundamentally deeming them both "terrorist groups which promote anarchism as a source of unrestricted freedom and happiness". Exceptions to this general external perception of the group include the [[Party Crashers]] and the [[The Halloween Party|Hallowe'en Party]], who are both organisation concerned deeply with the [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoer Jamboree]] and thus have made distinctions between the two groups. Many celebrants consider themselves to be a marginalised community. Importantly, the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' is in possession of the [[New Fun|obfestivir]] drug, which they use to convert [[partygoers]] and other victims of the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus into celebrants, thus adding to their ranks. Due to their unwillingness to release this medicine to the wider world, they have inadvertently become the enemies of many groups in the Backrooms. == Organisation == As aforementioned, the only official social structure maintained by the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' is a simplistic, ''laissez-faire'' governmental system, which [[the Party Creator]] states "exists only for the purpose of preserving some semblance of order 'mongst our nation, lest into chaos it descend, as the Jamboree of the filthy [[Sanguine Festivus|'gores]]". Divisions exist for legislation, healthcare, and law enforcement, among a few other purposes. |-|Senior Leadership= === The Concelebration === Senior leadership of '''New Fun''' is provided by the '''Concelebration of New Fun''', a parliamentary assembly composed of individuals who hold the title of '''concelebrant'''. Most concelebrants are [[New Fun|celebrants]] previously afflicted with [[Phenomenon 35-A|Jovial Syndrome]], allowing them to be more mental proficient and capable of sensible decision-making.See the notes on '''celebrants''' on the page for [[New Fun]]. The Concelebration has its congregatory headquarters in a spacious [[New Fun|Playhouse]] located in New Fun City, known as the '''Funhouse'''. ==== The Ceremony ==== The '''Ceremony''', officially the '''Royal Ceremony of New Fun''', is the cabinet of senior ministers which lead the Concelebration, composed of [[the Party Creator]] and six other concelebrants holding the title of '''Master of Ceremony''' (shortened to '''Emcee'''). Emcees hold power over the entirety of the New Fun government, able to employ or revoke legislative decisions at will, to amend the Celebrator's Constitution, and to appoint or dismiss new Boardheads and board members, decisions which can only be terminated by the opposition of the Party Creator or a pair of other Emcees. The Ceremony was established to emulate [[Caius Taggert|Sir Caius Taggert]]'s Co-Hosts. ==== Boardheads ==== The remainder of concelebrants who are not members of the Ceremony are known as '''Boardheads'''. As their title suggests, they oversee each board of the New Fun government subordinate to the Concelebration, with each of the three boards possessing four Boardheads. If left to their own devices by the Emcees, Boardheads have full directorial control over their respective divisions, able to appoint and dismiss members, alter or terminate ongoing operations, set new operations in motion, or create new subdivisions within their departments. |-|Legislature & Judicature= === Board of Aleators === The '''Board of Aleators''' ('''BoA''') is the division of the New Fun government responsible for law, policy and justice, comprising the justice system and the main legislative body of the group. Members of the board are termed '''aleators''', and are responsible for either enacting, amending and promulgating the laws of the nation, or for the administration of justice through court proceedings. Aleators who specifically take the role of magistrates or judges are known as '''court jesters'''. The BoA works closely in tandem with the '''Thin Blue Line''', whose members enforce the law implemented by the former. New Fun's legal policies and principles are not very extensive as compared to those of other nations, and largely are contained within the '''Celebrator's Constitution''', a document originally penned by [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]] at the founding of the group, with only minor emendations administered since then. Most policies are set in place to prevent the celebrant commoners interfering with governmental operations, and there appear to be no legal offences which may be defined as crimes against individual people. Courthouses established by the '''Board of Aleators''' are present in every city in New Fun, as well as many of the smaller settlements. The seat of the '''Jester's High Court''', New Fun's supreme court, is a [[New Fun|Playhouse]] at the heart of New Fun City known as the '''Aleatorium'''. |-|Healthcare & Pharmacy= === Board of Quacks === The '''Board of Quacks''' ('''BoQ''') is the division of the New Fun government responsible for medical, pharmaceutical and scientific application, especially public healthcare. Members of the board are known as '''quacksalvers''', and manage the diagnosis, prognosis and treatment of diseased or otherwise afflicted members of the celebrant public, as well as the production and development of medicine. Quacksalvers are the only associates of the New Fun governmental system who require official qualifications (typically a Medical Doctorate or PhD in Pharmacology) to take their role, as the Concelebration has deemed the presence of reliable medical practitioners and pharmacists important. The '''Board of Quacks''' is responsible for the continued production of the [[New Fun|obfestivir]] drug, the ingredients, development process and exact effects of which are kept strictly confidential by orders of the Ceremony. Boardhead '''Iris Allegra''', acting as representative for the BoQ, has stated that the Concelebration have no intention whatsoever of releasing the medicine for commercial use beyond the Celebrants' Jamboree. As with the courthouses of the '''Board of Aleators''', hospitals — usually comprising interconnected networks of [[New Fun|Rumpus Rooms]] and hallways — have been established by the '''BoQ''' all across major cities and some less significant settlements in [[New Fun]], so as to provide direct healthcare for all celebrants in need. The major hospital of New Fun, and the laboratory where obfestivir is rumoured to be produced, is called the '''Duckpond''', and is located in the major urban settlement of Discothèque. |-|Law Enforcement= === Thin Blue Line === The '''Thin Blue Line''' ('''TBL'''), sometimes simply referred to as the '''Line''', is the division of the New Fun government mainly in charge of the enforcement of law and justice, comprising equivalents to a police force, intelligence agency and carceral institution. Members of the Thin Blue Line are split into three categories: '''bluebottles''' of the '''Bluebottle Force''', who enforce the law and protect the public order; '''alphabets''' of the '''Abecedarian Correctional Staff''' ('''A.C.S.'''), who serve as custodians in the national prison of the '''Abecedarium'''; and '''skylarks''' of the '''Skylark Intelligence and Espionage Agency''' ('''S.I.E.A.'''), who act as scouts or spies, journeying beyond the borders of [[New Fun]] to garner information for the Concelebration. Typically, '''bluebottles''' patrol the streets of New Fun settlements, ensuring members of the celebrant public do not break any one of the few laws which are stated in the Celebrator's Constitution, although they may also be exclusively dispatched to track down and capture lawbreakers or unwanted invaders.These invaders are typically [[partygoers]]. '''Skylarks''' mostly move in groups of three to four, and are primarily sent to infiltrate [[Level Fun]], collect information on the operations of the [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoer Jamboree]], and bring back [[partygoers]] to convert into celebrants. Of the four Boardheads assigned to the '''Thin Blue Line''', one is appointed as the '''Greenbottle''' (chief of the Bluebottle Force); another is appointed as the '''Warden Syllabary''' (chief of the Abecedarian Correctional Staff); and the other two are appointed as '''Albatrosses''', directors of the S.I.E.A. == Colonies & Settlements == |-|New Fun= The following is a short overview of some of '''New Fun''''s politically and culturally significant settlements in [[New Fun]]. Note that it is not a comprehensive list. === New Fun City === '''New Fun City''', sometimes abbreviated as '''NFC''', is the capital city of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' and the most populated of its settlements, home to over 5,000 celebrants and comprising over 30 square kilometres' worth of [[New Fun|Rumpus Rooms]]. It is the site of the expansive [[New Fun|Jungle Gym]] called the Amazon, and of three major [[New Fun|Playhouses]], these being the Funhouse, the Aleatorium, and the Blake Opera House. In recent years, a statue called the Autark has been erected in the town square of NFC, recalling the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Statue_of_Liberty Statue of Liberty] of New York, USA, and serving as a reminder of the celebrants' liberation from the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus. As well as being the de jure capital of New Fun, '''New Fun City''' is also the political heart of the nation thanks to the presence of the aforementioned Funhouse and the Aleatorium. As such, due to the numerous administrative factors which the city's security is critical to, a large delegate of around 500 celebrants from the Bluebottle Force has been stationed around its perimeter to deter possible intruders. === Discothèque === '''Discothèque''', sometimes shortened to '''Disco''', is a large city located near New Fun City, with a population of approximately 4,390 celebrants and an area of around 40 square kilometres, most of which are comprised of [[New Fun|Rumpus Rooms]] and [[New Fun|Nightclubs]]. Named hence for the surprising amount of Nightclubs to be found within its territory, Discothèque additionally is the site of the Duckpond), as well as the Nickelsworth Memorial Arcade.The Nickelsworth Memorial Arcade was named for a former Co-Host and a friend of [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]], Skyler B. Nickelsworth, who perished [[The Party Pooper|in 1968]]. The city is the base of operations of the Board of Quacks. === Scarletsburg === '''Scarletsburg''', sometimes shortened to '''Scarls''' or nicknamed the '''Red Town''', is a moderate-sized city with a population of approximately 2,100 celebrants and an area of around 25 square kilometres, which is mostly comprised of [[New Fun|Rumpus Rooms]], [[New Fun|Penny Arcades]] and the Crimsonholt [[New Fun|Jungle Gym]]. An interesting subculture has flourished in this city, as it is known that the Scarletsburgers have taken to painting the streets and buildings of their conurbation with a bright scarlet paint reminiscent of fresh blood. The paint is renewed every six months during a noteworthy festival known as the '''Robigo'''.From Latin ''robigo'' ("rust"). === Holtside === '''Holtside''' is a small town at the edge of a wide [[New Fun|Jungle Gym]] termed the Pelorius, with a population of approximately 100 celebrants and an area of around 9 square kilometres. Despite its humble appearance it is most infamous as the seat of the Abecedarium, the singular carceral institution and correctional facility in [[New Fun]]. The few celebrants who have ever broken New Fun law are isolated in the prison for a length of time appropriated by a court jester from the Board of Aleators, guarded securely by alphabets of the Abecedarian Correctional Staff. The residents of Holtside are known mostly to be cannibals. |-|Other levels= There are four bases belonging to the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' which are not located within [[New Fun]]. Two of these are known to be situated in [[Level Fun]], likely as outposts of the Skylark Intelligence and Espionage Agency, although there is no definite public information about them otherwise. The other two bases, meanwhile, have been more public about their operations. === The Moviegoer's === '''The Moviegoer's''' is an outpost in [[Level 40]] consisting of around 30 celebrants, including five governmental officials, their aim being to expand the territory of '''New Fun''' beyond [[New Fun]]. Apparently, much of their time is spent engaging in "movie nights", which is said to involve the members of the colony crowding around a screen to view films taken from the shelves of the store. Due to the anomalous properties of the [[New Fun|celebrant]] mind, none of these viewings have caused any detrimental psychological effect on any member of the outpost. === Notherfun === '''Notherfun''' is an outpost in [[Level 26]] consisting of around 45 celebrants, including six governmental officials, who are attempting to construct a habitable colony in a sizeable area of the level's Empty Rooms. Doors leading to their colony have been marked with a crude balloon drawn in red or purple crayon; it is advised to avoid these, as members of Notherfun are known to be extremely hostile to outsiders. Many of Notherfun's inhabitants hail from the town of Holtside, and thus several members are known to have already been cannibalised during a heated argument. == History == === Background (2000-2003) === In 2000, [[the Party Creator]] set out to find a cure to [[Sanguine Festivus]]. The Creator abducted thirty chemists and twenty doctors from the [[Backrooms Red Cross]], as well as Euphoria Kronenscheldt,The head of the contemporary Co-Hosts, who also happens to be an accomplished medico. and forced them to develop a drug which would eliminate the pathogen. Eventually, the team invented the miracle medicine obfestivir, and after testing it on several infected lab-rats, Blake dosed himself with the drug and was cured of the virus. Supposedly, the sheer joy of freedom that the '''Party Creator''' felt upon being released from the virus's clutches was enough to convince him that this was in fact the ''true'' path to happiness. Thereafter came the beginning of the great obfestivir operation, and the founding of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' in hopes of building a power strong enough to overthrow [[Caius Taggert]] — Blake had apparently offered [[the Party Pooper]] a place as the second founding father of the new jamboree, but the latter had firmly refused. After discovering [[New Fun|a new section]] of [[Level Fun]] which had [[Splits|split]] off from the parent level some years ago, [[the Party Creator]] chose to make this his new base of operations. In 2001, he dubbed the sub-level ''New Fun'', and began to dispatch agents in protective clothing to the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]] to persuade and recruit [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]] to his cause. The obfestivir operation managed to convince five hundred partygoers to cure and reform themselves, transfiguring them into the followers he affectionately termed the [[New Fun|celebrants]]. In 2003, the Euphoric Wars began in earnest, and [[Caius Taggert]] was forced to rally [[partygoers]] from far and wide to resist the [[Party Crashers]], under his psychic influence. As it were, it seems that to spread a web of Achospic manipulation so wide inadvertently lessened the effect of the mind-control upon each individual partygoer; this thus allowed [[the Party Creator]] and his newly-engaged agents to more easily reform thousands more partygoers into celebrants in secret, as all the while the war between the Crashers and the Jamboree raged on in the limelight. === Founding (2004) === On 1st April 2004, [[the Party Creator]] allied with six of his wisest celebrants to form the '''Royal Ceremony of New Fun''', and they decided, with the purpose of forging a shared cultural identity amongst the celebrants, to found a nation: thus the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' was formed, and these seven founding fathers dubbed the "'''Emcees'''". The Emcees created the three minimalistic departments of New Fun government and penned the Celebrator's Constitution, setting out a simple legal system which prohibited nothing but interference with governmental procedure. Blake collaborated with Elias Payton Czerny, the most musically inclined of the Emcees, to compose the national anthem "There Lieth a Dawn"; he also designed the Dawn Standard, which is New Fun's national flag, and from his coat of arms he retrieved the national motto, '''''Ex Crepusculo Aurora'''''. === Early Years (2005-2008) === The early years of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' were tumultuous. Violence and murder ravaged the halls of [[New Fun]], and the daily life of a celebrant was comparable to the life of a [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoer]]. In an attempt to mellow things, [[the Party Creator]] began to release propagandistic material, especially posters and stage performances, to the general public, trying to convince them to stop behaving in an animalistic fashion. However, four years went by before the populace finally began to pull itself out of barbarianism. === ''Naissance'' (2009-2015) === After the populace finally calmed, the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' experienced a cultural proliferation. The population of the nation increased steadily, as celebrants continued to trickle in through obfestivir, and yet more were born as a result of reproduction amongst the populace; the people formed lasting communities, established settlements all over [[New Fun]], and made New Fun a stabilised state in spite of its lacking economy, military, or overall infrastructure. Moreover, following an initial surge in propaganda issued by the New Fun government, creative expression experienced a resounding boom (see ''Culture §§ "Theatre" & "Music"''). === [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]] (2016) === As the years went on, '''New Fun''' grew into a prosperous state, and its numbers increased so greatly that the celebrants now rivalled the might of the [[Caius Taggert|Partygoer Jamboree]], whose ranks were weakened as a result of the Euphoric Wars. In February 2016, [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]] ravaged the Backrooms, plunging [[New Fun]] into darkness and also bringing about the end of the Euphoric Wars. Knowing that the immobilisation of the [[Party Crashers]] meant there was now nothing to stop [[Caius Taggert]]'s forces from multiplying exponentially, the New Fun government proposed to strike the Partygoer Jamboree before it was too late. === Modernity === The celebrants of '''New Fun''' continue to exist in their paradise, protected by the thresholds of [[New Fun]], which usually lead entering wanderers to a deserted part of the level, far from any celebrant settlement. Beyond the walls of the sub-level, however, many groups have formed an offensive alliance against the Celebrants' Jamboree with the intent to destroy them and wrest the obfestivir drug from their control, at the forefront of this Misers' Alliance being the [[Party Crashers]], the Anti-Entity Group, and the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation]]. The association awaits New Fun's inevitable war with the Partygoer Jamboree before formally declaring war on them. === Future === As of 2026, war is expected to break out between '''New Fun''' and the [[Caius Taggert|Partygoer Jamboree]] within the next year. As both sides are determined to be evenly matched, the victor of the inevitable conflict remains uncertain; however, as a representative from the [[Party Crashers]] says, "should the Celebrants' Jamboree prevail, the world would rejoice at once at the toppling of [[Caius Taggert|a tyrant]]; rapturous fanfare would be heard in the dingiest corners of the Backrooms, celebrations would be held in every remotest level and darkest location, even the Crashers would, for once, throw a party in triumph at the end of [[Sanguine Festivus|parties]] — and then, of course, we would all sober up and realise that there's still another bloody jamboree we've yet to take care of. God bless the Misers' Alliance!" On the other hand, political collapse within the Celebrants' Jamboree itself has been speculated by some to be drawing near. As Mr Andrew of the [[A.A.E.G.]] states, "It's a surprise the celebrants lasted so long with basically no laws. If war doesn't end them, their lack of social structure will. But then again, how ''did'' they last for so long?" == Symbols == {{Simple Image Block|image=New Fun Flag.png|caption=The flag of New Fun.}} === Flag === The national flag of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''', sometimes referred to as the '''Dawn Standard''', was designed by [[the Party Creator]] in 2004 to represent the nation. It consists of a horizontal bicolour of purple above white, overlaid in the canton by an overturned burgundy pentagram, and with a coral pink rising sun centred over the bottom of the white band. The pentagram, representing the "satanic" tendencies of the [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoer Jamboree]] as well as the stars of the "Crimson Dusk", is upside-down to symbolise triumph over the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus's control; the rising sun represents a new beginning and the light of the "Blessed Dawn". The colours have different symbolic meanings: purple stands for wealth and fulfilment, in alignment with the luxuries and spiritual fulfilment promised by the personal freedom and "happiness" offered by the nation; white stands for purity, peace and cleanliness, portraying the celebrants' alleviation of the "loathsome" virus, the peace they have made with the chaos of the world by embracing it, and their supposed perfect nature as compared to other, "lower" beings. Red was suggested as a third colour to be included, symbolic of the celebrants' revolution against [[Caius Taggert|the Party Host]] and his minions, but [[the Party Creator]] objected to this on the account that he did not want his people portrayed as being weaker than the [[partygoers]] as to have to rebel against them. Some have argued that the colours of the flag are also representative of [[the Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]] himself: purple being the shade of the garb which he wears; and white representing his surname, "Blake", which is derived from Old English ''blāc'' ("pale"). Furthermore, the coral pink of the rising sun has been speculated to represent the ocean, with which Blake's mythological namesake was greatly associated. No reliable evidence to support this outlook has been found. === Anthem === The national anthem of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' is titled "'''There Lieth a Dawn'''", and was composed in mid-2004 by the late concelebrant and composer '''Elias Payton Czerny''', with lyrics by [[the Party Creator]]. The lyrics are in English, written in an anachronistic manner meant to mimic old-fashioned, Christian patriotic songs from [[the Frontrooms]]; this style has notably inspired much of the structure belonging to the Carou-Speak pidgin. The full lyrics of "'''There Lieth a Dawn'''", which have not been altered since its composition, are as follows. ::When Thaumas Blake from death returned,
::With mind unfray'd, and soul unburn'd;
::When Thaumas Blake, o wondrous he,
::Turned 'gainst the Jamboree;
::He filled the pots of herbs and leaves,
::He found a cure to the disease
::Which for so long has plagued us all:
::Now we are free of gall.
::We never care for anyone,
::For evermore, we're having fun;
::Amongst the balls and swings and slides,
::Layeth our nation wide. ::After the bright white sun's mundanity,  
::Beyond the scarlet dusk's insanity,
::There lieth a dawn,
::A brand new dawn;
::Above all of the woe and gore. ::When Thaumas Blake to Satan spake,
::And pulled us from his Fiery Lake;
::Thus Thaumas Blake, that wondrous chap,
::Brought to us much good hap.   ::After the bright white sun's mundanity,
::Beyond the scarlet dusk's insanity,
::There lieth a dawn,
::A brand new dawn,
::Above ideas of peace and war,
::Shining, blinding, binding —
::''Ex crepusculo aurora!'' Sometimes, a shortened version which removes the first refrain and fourth verse is used. {{Simple Image Block|image=Thaumas Blake CoA.png|caption=[[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]]'s personal coat of arms, including the New Fun national motto.}} === Motto === The official national motto of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' is the Latin phrase '''''Ex Crepusculo Aurora''''', usually translated as "From dusk cometh dawn". Taken from [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]]'s personal coat of arms, the motto inspired the terminology of the '''Tribeatic Model''' and also constitutes the the final line of "There Lieth a Dawn", the national anthem. According to the Party Creator, the phrase is in reference to the fact that nobody can achieve ontological perfection (i.e. a celebrant) without first contracting [[Sanguine Festivus]]. Other mottos have been suggested by members of the Concelebration, including "For evermore, we're having fun" and "''Sanati sumus''".Latin for "We are cured". However, ''Ex Crepusculo Aura'' eventually won out against all competition. === Balloons === Balloons are dictated under the '''Celebrator's Constitution''' as a national symbol of the '''Celebrants' Jamboree'''. They are symbolic of joy, celebration, hope, and the carefree spirit of childhood, thus embodying the virtues which New Fun professes to stand for. Moreover, balloons are often representative of significant milestones, in this case [[the Party Creator]]'s vanquishment of the [[Sanguine Festivus|Sanguine Festivus Virus]] through the [[New Fun|obfestivir]] drug. Thaumas Blake himself is often witnessed traversing the halls of [[New Fun]] with a balloon in his hand. == Culture == === Social Norms === As aforementioned, the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' promotes freedom of speech and action (sometimes defined as "lawless and somewhat anarchistic behaviour", although this is debatable) among its citizenry, officially termed as "providing [their] celebrants a limitless [...] freedom and happiness". As a result, celebrants are typically violent and hostile toward outsiders, and even toward each other should a conflict arise, and they are more liable to display "unorthodox" behaviour (e.g. cannibalism, unprovoked murder, etc.) in comparison with humans.Note that such behaviour is, at present, still relatively rare across the population as a whole. It is a common rule of thumb that those who have intrude, whether purposefully or inadvertently, upon a New Fun settlement should not expect to survive for more than five minutes. Due to the populace's lack of strict governmental control, celebrant society is chaotic and extremely diverse, and as such there are no widely recognised customs across the entire nation. In certain communities, on the other hand, there may be specific practices followed in daily life; these include the city of '''Scarletsburg''', where all structures and clothing must be coloured red, and the small municipality of '''St Blakeswick''', where the daily greeting is infamously "Thaumas Blake, o wondrous he". === Isolect === Across the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''', the most commonly spoken language is English, although it is mostly configurated in the form of a pidgin-basilect which has been termed '''Carou-Speak'''. This lect has several major differences to orthodox, everyday English, most of which are the result of the anachronistic style in which the lyrics of "There Lieth a Dawn"The only official, public English work of New Fun, and thus the only definitive exposure to English many non-Anglophone celebrants may have. is composed. Below are listed some of the major idiosyncrasies boasted by Carou-Speak. * Indirect objects are often placed before the verb. E.g., ''"I to him gived the book."'' * The verbal suffix "-eth" is sometimes used as an indicator of second-person or third-person. E.g., ''"If you payeth me, the same I'll do.'' * Direct objects are sometimes placed before the subject. ''"That information we know 'lready."'' * "Like" as a preposition is usually replaced by "as". E.g., ''"He toward me flied, as a cheetah."'' * The "-ed" verbal suffix is often used as an indicator of past tense, regardless of whether this is grammatically correct. E.g., ''"I maked these laws, idiots!"'' * In many common polysyllabic words beginning with a vowel, this initial vowel sound is dropped. E.g., ''"Don't you 'gainst me start."'' * Occasionally, articles or possessive adjectives are dropped. E.g., ''"Constitution I've readed, how 'bout you?"'' * Archaic terminology, such as "lest", "unto", and "anon", is sometimes used. E.g., ''"I'll do it 'non, you can of that be sure."'' ==== Slang ==== Several examples of common Carou-Speak slang terms are listed below.
{| class="fandom-table" |- | '''Term''' | '''Meaning''' | '''Notes''' |- | az | to like, to appreciate. | From "as" being used in place of "like". See ''Back-NET Culture''. |- | celeborn | a second-generation celebrant; a celebrant with celebrant parents. | Blend of "celebrant" and "born". |- | concel | a person who overthinks things and is tiresome to be around. | Clipping of "concelebrant", in reference to their higher mental acuity. Possible association with "incel". |- | creppy | an insane person; a [[partygoer]] or [[Phenomenon 35|wretch]]. | Clipping of Latin ''crepusculo'' ("from dusk"), from ''Ex Crepusculo Aurora''. |- | eggie | a member of the A.E.G. | From "A.E.G.", on the pattern of "meggie" from "[[M.E.G.]]". |- | elpart | a member of the [[The Halloween Party|Hallowe'en Party]]. | Condensation of "Hallowe'en Party", altered upon association with the word "Hell", before the initial "h" was dropped. |- | firstgen | a first-generation celebrant; a celebrant who was originally infected with [[Sanguine Festivus]]. | Condensation of "first generation". |- | 'gore | a [[partygoer]]. | Clipping of "partygoer". Possible association with "gore". |- | seller | a [[New Fun|celebrant]]. | Clipping of "celebrant". |- | 'swell | as well, too. | Contraction of "as well". |}
=== Theatre === Despite the common (and largely incorrect) notion that "celebrants are illiterate", a number of famous dramatic productions have emerged from the '''New Fun''' populace, and a few of these have even been reproduced in theatres beyond the nation based on clips shared on [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Backvideo]] and scripts leaked on [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] and the [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Free Backrooms Forum]]. Some of these productions are propagandistic plays commissioned by the Concelebration in the early days of the Celebrants' Jamboree, but many others are simply to product of creative freedom and expression amongst the celebrant population. Below is a list of the more famous productions from the '''Celebrants' Jamboree'''.
{| class="fandom-table" |- | style="width: 20%;" | '''Title''' | '''Synopsis''' | '''Notes''' |- | ''Blake'' (2008) | A dramatisation of [[the Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]]'s life, including insights into his time with [[Caius Taggert]] and his rise to power as the Party Creator. | Directed by Peter Spindelssohn. Written by Arthur Sakharine. Music by Elias P. Czerny. Commissioned by the Concelebration as a propaganda play. First performed at the Blake Opera House. |- | ''Not So Gracious'' (2010) | A political drama wherein [[Caius Taggert]] finds his influence crumbling and is eventually turned against by his own Co-Hosts, who kill and cannibalise him. | Directed by Peter Spindelssohn. Written by Kate T. Chirkova and Walter Neydis. Music by Hans N. Thiar. Commissioned by the Concelebration as a propaganda play. First performed at the Blake Opera House. |- | ''The Art of the Deal'' (2013) | A musical detailing the perilous journey of the [[the Beast of Level 5]] (here called "Therion") from the waters of [[Level 7]] to the purgatory of [[Level 5]]. | Directed by Oliver Garrison. Written by Thomas T. Thomas. Music by Nadir Zenith. Based on the ancient epic poem [[The Beast of Level 5|''Phrikeid'']], as well as other classical depictions of [[the Beast of Level 5]]. First performed at the Royal Blakespeare Playhouse. |- | ''The Purple Guy'' (2015) | A thriller reimagining [[the Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]] as a serial killer, who dons a yellow partygoer raiment to murder the incumbent Co-Hosts and [[Caius Taggert]]. | Directed by Amanda Jacobson. Written by Michael Julius Lee. Music by Nadir Zenith. First performed at the Royal Blakespeare Playhouse. |- | ''Doctor Impostor'' (2019) | A medical drama detailing a fictional version of [[the Backrooms]] wherein [[Asclepius Ang|Dr Asclepius Ang]], [[Phenomenon 303|pathostatic]] [[Entity 10|skin-stealer]], finds his capacity for emotions restored by [[Entity 444|Dr Astrid Nightingale]], and suddenly must face the consequences of what he has done in the past. | Directed by Oliver Garrison. Written by Ashley Howardson. Music by Hans N. Thiar and Melvin Eurus. Based on the book series ''The Capgras Doctor''. First performed at the Palais Dreary. |- | ''Creppy Crawly'' (2019) | A play about Cecil LeBrant, a fictional celebrant, who finds that he dislikes life in the [[CJNF]] and departs to seek his fortune, posing as a human. However, upon emerging into the wider Wake, he faces discrimination due to his origins. | Directed by Lynda C. McGregor. Written by Walter Neydis and Charles Chevron. Music by Lawrence Padrino. First performed at the Crimson Lane Theatre. |- | ''Hey Diddle Diddle'' (2024) | A revisionist dark fantasy musical set in the world of [[Caius Taggert]]'s production ''Fiddlesticks!'', following the life story of Megeus, the character designated as the main antagonist in the original play. | Directed by Peter Spindelssohn. Written by Ashley Howardson and Sherman Roberts. Music by Cedric Walker. Inspired by [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] musical ''Wicked''; based on the musical ''Fiddlesticks!''. First performed at the Blake Opera House. |}
=== Music === Arguably, the subsect of the arts which the celebrants of '''New Fun''' are most inclined to is music. Multiple instances of pop and funk genre songs written and sung by celebrant artists have topped the charts in recent years, and a select few musical numbers from famous celebrant-written plays have gone viral after the musicals' restaging beyond the Celebrants' Jamboree. Below is a list of the top songs produced by celebrants which have taken the world by storm, on occasion even trumping the carefully crafted [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] parodies of [[Caius Taggert|the Party Host]].
{| class="fandom-table" |- | style="width: 20%;" | '''Song''' | '''Artist(s)''' | '''Notes''' |- | "Lured Away" (2013) | Vocals by Claude DeBosse ([[Entity 20|Res]]) ''et al''. Music and lyrics by Nadir Zenith. | From the musical stage production ''The Art of the Deal''. "Lured Away" involves the mischievous minor character [[Entity 20|Res]] cycling through his thirty-six transformationsIn ''The Art of the Deal'', [[The Beast of Level 5|Therion]] and [[Entity 20|Res]] both have thirty-six transformations, in reference to the former's depiction as a shapeshifting demon-king in Chinese mythology. as he attempts to convince his brother, [[The Beast of Level 5|Therion]], to stay in [[Level 7|Thalassa's Realm]] and not to be "lured away" by the call of [[Level 5|Terror Hotel]]. The number has been praised by various critics as "the best goofy sidekick song in modern musical theatre, reminiscent of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Under_the_Sea 'Under the Sea']." It hit No. 6 on the [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Mercury Retrograde Chart]] in early 2015. |- | "Catfish" (2017) | Vocals, music and lyrics by Monica Cody. | From the studio album ''Right Brained''. "Catfish" is a country-pop song composed by popular celebrant singer Cody. As the song's title suggests, its lyrics tackle the concept of "catfishing" — using a fictitious online persona for deceptive purposes — with the song's narrator deceiving an innocent victim for his money via the pig butchering scam, only to develop an emotional attachment to the victim and begin to reconsider her prior decisions. The track topped the charts in early 2018, hitting No. 1 on the [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Mercury Retrograde Chart]] and prompting Cody to re-release it as a single two months later. |- | "Blake It Off" (2018) | Vocals and lyrics by Kobolda Hastiff. Music by Ian D. Griffiths. | "Blake It Off" is a dance-pop single, composed by Hastiff and Griffiths with the intention of being as catchy as possible. Its lyrics are mockingly propagandistic, mainly related to slandering [[Caius Taggert]]; the song uses [[the Party Creator|Blake]]'s name in its main refrain. Famously, [[the Party Creator]] sentenced Hastiff to prison for one day due to "defamatory actions", but reportedly enjoyed the song so much that he ordered the alphabets to treat the prisoner as they would treat a queen. The track went viral on [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Backvideo]] in 2019, hitting No. 3 on the [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Mercury Retrograde Chart]] in 2021, and is popularly used as a background song in videos to this day. |- | "You Dat Serpent" (2021) | Vocals, music and lyrics by Revellution. | From the studio album ''Let There Be Light''. "You Dat Serpent" is an electro-funk song by popoular celebrant band Revellution. Its strong beat has been described as "a hypnotising groove". Its lyrics, like those of every track in ''Let There Be Light'', use biblical terminology to convey its hedonistic message of shunning the passionless and the monotonous. In this case, the Serpent from the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garden_of_Eden Garden of Eden] is used as a metaphor for killjoys and boring people. The track went viral on [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Backvid]] almost as soon as it released, and hit No. 4 on the [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Mercury Retrograde Chart]] in 2022. |- | "A Tyrant Is Born" (2024) | Vocals by Robert P. Casca (Deacon) and Erwin Butcherson (Celious). Music and lyrics by Cedric Walker. | From the musical stage production ''Hey Diddle Diddle''. "A Tyrant Is Born" is the opening number of the play, sung by Celious and Deacon to contextualise the death of the protagonist Megeus, as a part of the frame narrative before the story flashes back to Megeus's early years. Driven by its theatrical nature, the song went viral on [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Backvideo]] in late 2024, hitting No. 2 on the [[Extended BACK-NET Website List|Mercury Retrograde Chart]] a few months later. |}
On another hand, the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' has also produced a non-governmental orchestra called the '''Celebrator's Philharmonic Symphony''', which performs monthly at the Blake Opera House in New Fun City and has also accomplished several tours around [[New Fun]]. They are most well known for their renditions of famous classical pieces, although they also stage original compositions by celebrant composers such as their conductor, Maximilian Czerny.The brother of the late Emcee, Elias Payton Czerny. === Religion === ==== Tribeatism ==== The main religion in the '''Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun''' is '''Tribeatism''',Derived from ''tri-'' ("three") and Latin ''beatus'' ("blessed"). a belief system portraying celebrants as the most perfect form attainable by a human being. It states three stages required to achieve perfection in a structure alluded to as the Tribeatic Model, these being the "Bright White Sun" (representing regular human life, its mundanity and monotony), the "Scarlet Dusk" (representing infection by the [[Sanguine Festivus|Sanguine Festivus Virus]] and the insanity it brings), and finally the "Blessed Dawn" (representing being cured via [[New Fun|obfestivir]] and achieving celebranthood). Effectively, the religion declares that "it is darkest before dawn"; only by enduring pain and suffering as a carrier of SFV, can one achieve spiritual fulfilment. The principles of Tribeatism are firmly established in celebrants since the moment they are transformed using the obfestivir drug, and are subsequently reinforced by political speeches and propaganda delivered by the government, to ensure that celebrants remain loyal to the '''Celebrants' Jamboree'''. As evidenced by the patriotic behaviour displayed during the Dance-Battle of Discothèque, this government initiative was effective. ==== Christianity ==== Culture in '''New Fun''' has been influenced by Christianity to some extent, especially due to the fact that [[the Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]] himself identifies as partially Christian. The state's national anthem, for example, uses the biblical elements of Satan and the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lake_of_fire Lake of Fire] as metaphors for [[Caius Taggert]] and the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus, and the Party Host is sometimes spitefully referred to within the Celebrants' Jamboree as the "Antichrist". === [[Phenomenon 2|Back-NET]] Culture === ==== "Az" ==== In 2014, an operative from the Anti-Entity Group made a post on [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] which ended with the line, "Make sure to like this post... or if you're an aleck,"Aleck" is a shortening of "alacrit", an exonym for celebrants which may be seen as derogatory. you can ass it." This was intended to be a discriminatory remark in reference to how Carou-Speak often replaces the word "like", in a prepositional context, with "as", to mimic an archaic way of speaking inherited from the national anthem. In response to the post, [[the Party Creator]] replied, "Yes eggie I have azzed your post 'tis hilarioso", and the population of '''New Fun''' have ironically adopted "to az" as a slang term synonymous with "to like" or "to appreciate". == Political Relations == === [[Backrooms Red Cross|The Backrooms Association of the Red Cross]] === The [[Backrooms Red Cross]] does not maintain a good relationship with the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''', mostly on the account of the Jamboree's consistent refusal to reveal the nature of the [[New Fun|obfestivir]] miracle-medicine to them; although some of the enmity also stems from [[the Party Creator]] abducting thirty chemists and twenty doctors from the Red Cross, forcing them to develop the medication, and subsequently drugging them with [[Object 33|memory flame]]. Only one of these chemists and three of these doctors have recovered their memory after being prescribed [[Object 33|mnemonous dicorruptide]], although they curiously cannot recall anything of obfestivir beyond that they assisted in its creation. === [[The Halloween Party|The Hallowe'en Party]] === The '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' and the denizens of the [[The Halloween Party|Hallowe'en Party]] (referred to as "elparts" in Carou-Speak) primarily maintain neutral political relations, both [[groups]] agreeing not to interfere with each other's operations. There have been records of celebrants appearing peacefully at the festivities of the Crimson Carnival. Due to the main religion of the Party being Satanism, [[Clyde Nelson]], the organisation's cultmaster, has expressed amusement at the New Fun national anthem using Satan as a metaphor for [[Caius Taggert]], humorously stating at the fifth Crimson Carnival that "Caius is great, but he isn't ''that'' great". More active political collaboration between New Fun and the Party has been discussed. === The Misers' Alliance === The '''Misers' Alliance''' is an alliance of around ten [[groups]] (including A.E.G., the [[Party Crashers]], [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]], [[M.E.G.]] etc.) which are cooperating in an attempt destroy the '''Celebrants' Jamboree''', largely motivated by their unwillingness to release obfestivir to the wider world. [[The Party Creator]] has largely shown disregard toward the Misers' Alliance, stating that the [[partygoers]] are his main concern. The [[Party Crashers]] have convinced the other members of the alliance to await the conclusion of the imminent war between the [[Caius Taggert|Partygoer Jamboree]] and New Fun before assailing the latter, with the intent that the Celebrants' Jamboree could potentially triumph over Taggert's forces, and two birds could be killed with one stone. In the meantime, operations are underway to discover a suitable entrance to [[New Fun]] from which to directly assail a major city. === [[Caius Taggert|The Partygoer Jamboree]] === The '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' harbours paramount hatred toward the Partygoer Jamboree as a polity, mostly due to their disagreement with the rule of [[Caius Taggert]], whom they see as a totalitarian and tyrannical dictator who has induced millions to join his ranks via unfair and duplicitous manipulation. Within the Celebrants' Jamboree, Sir Taggert is commonly referred to as the "Mogul of the Virus" or, more spitefully, the "Antichrist". Toward [[Sanguine Festivus|the people of the Partygoer Jamboree]], however, the Concelebration of New Fun takes a more "sympathetic" approach largely influenced by Tribeatistic beliefs, periodically sending skylarks of the S.I.E.A. to [[Level Fun]] with the purpose of bringing back [[partygoers]], who are subsequently dosed with [[New Fun|obfestivir]].The exact procedure of this "bringing" of [[partygoers]] to [[New Fun]] is yet undisclosed. However, it is known that Thaumas Blake intially began the obfestivir operation by persuading partygoers to convert to celebranthood, instead of utilising force or violence. As of early 2026, tensions between the two [[groups]] have been rising for a decade, and many believe they are on the brink of war. [[The Party Creator|Thaumas Blake]] himself has stated that he plans to strike the Partygoer Jamboree before their numbers have a chance to significantly recover from the [[Party Crashers|Euphoric Wars]]. [[Caius Taggert]], on the other hand, does not seem concerned about '''New Fun''' on the surface, although when questioned about possible conflict between the two groups, he answered that there "is a good chance that this'll blossom into the finest, bloodiest spectacle the Wake's seen since the Bloodbath o'Cluster I". === [[Party Crashers|The Party Crashers]] === The '''Celebrants' Jamboree''' and the [[Party Crashers]] do not have as much enmity between them as is present between the [[Caius Taggert|Partygoer Jamboree]] and the latter, although there are significant political tensions between the two groups, especially since the formation of the Misers' Alliance. The Director of the Crashers has publicly stated that "these 'celebrants' may not be infected with [[Sanguine Festivus|the virus]], and we respect that, but nonetheless they are still joyous revelling freaks who pose a significant threat to the well-being of humankind in the Backrooms. We do not purport to associate with the Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun, even though they are perhaps the most powerful opposers of [[Caius Taggert|the Party Host]] behind us. I trust that this decision will be the wisest the Crashers have ever made." == Footnotes ==
''Guess who stole a pint of obfestivir...''
Neurochemicals. I knew it. I knew it, [[The Party Creator|Blake]]. How ironic that your entire operation was just brainwashing, with a bit of virucide on the side... I doubt you knew that obfestivir induces fervent loyalty. But how couldn't you? Are you so deluded that you didn't realise you have become an autocrat? And it's not just the medicine either. To think I believed for a second your foolish idea would work, even if I opposed it! What was the first thing you did with that governmental power of yours, Blake? Propaganda. I was right all along. Power does strange things to the human mind, Blake. You can hate those who brainwash their subjects to maintain their power, and still brainwash your own subjects to maintain yours. Don't tell me that you "need" your power so that you can put a "righteous" end to the Party Host. Every ruler has a reason why they "need" their power. And what was that about not intending to be a dictator? The second generation of celebrants, the ones who haven't ever been dosed with the drug... Consider them, Blake. There's no neurochemicals in ''their'' brains, or at least not in any great quantity. They won't be fervently loyal to you. They may revolt. They will be your downfall. Unless, of course, if you exercise your power over the masses. Is that really something you will never do? Not a chance. Power does strange things to the human mind, Blake. Power can turn you into the very thing you sought to destroy. ... Huh, that's strange. There appears to be an unidentified substance in this sample.
